After the third SPACE:
- Node 1: color=W (white), boundary=[[24, 44], [24, 47], [28, 47], [28, 44]], pixels=16 - the frame is now white again (was G before)
- Node 2: color=b (blue), boundary=[[25, 45], [25, 46], [26, 46], [26, 45]] - blue square still there
- Player (N) at rows 29-31, cols 44-47
So the pattern for the right target frame is:
- Initial: c (charcoal)
- 1st SPACE: W (white) - 16 pixels (hollow 4x4 frame)
- 2nd SPACE: G (dark gray) - 12 pixels (hollow 4x3 frame?)
- 3rd SPACE: W (white) - 16 pixels again
The frame alternates between W and G with each SPACE. The blue square remains unchanged.

- Three square targets (charcoal frame, blue center)
- One horizontal bar target (charcoal frame, gray interior)
- Player (green) at bottom center
- Pink bar at bottom (timer)
Maybe the game is about the player moving to touch/overlap with the blue squares to collect them? Let me try moving the player directly into one of the blue squares.
The player is currently at rows 29-31, cols 44-47. The right target's blue square is at rows 25-26, cols 45-46. I need to move UP to reach it.
Actually wait, the player is already quite close. Let me try moving UP to overlap with the blue square.

</parameter>
</function>
